CURLINFO;\n\n/* CURLINFO_RESPONSE_CODE is the new name for the option previously known as\n   CURLINFO_HTTP_CODE */\n#define CURLINFO_HTTP_CODE CURLINFO_RESPONSE_CODE\n\ntypedef enum {\n  CURLCLOSEPOLICY_NONE, /* first, never use this */\n\n  CURLCLOSEPOLICY_OLDEST,\n  CURLCLOSEPOLICY_LEAST_RECENTLY_USED,\n  CURLCLOSEPOLICY_LEAST_TRAFFIC,\n  CURLCLOSEPOLICY_SLOWEST,\n  CURLCLOSEPOLICY_CALLBACK,\n\n  CURLCLOSEPOLICY_LAST /* last, never use this */\n} curl_closepolicy;\n\n#define CURL_GLOBAL_SSL (1<<0) /* no purpose since 7.57.0 */\n#define CURL_GLOBAL_WIN32 (1<<1)\n#define CURL_GLOBAL_ALL (CURL_GLOBAL_SSL|CURL_GLOBAL_WIN32)\n#define CURL_GLOBAL_NOTHING 0\n#define CURL_GLOBAL_DEFAULT CURL_GLOBAL_ALL\n#define CURL_GLOBAL_ACK_EINTR (1<<2)\n\n\n/*****************************************************************************\n * Setup defines, protos etc for the sharing stuff.\n */\n\n/* Different data locks for a single share */\ntypedef enum {\n  CURL_LOCK_DATA_NONE = 0,\n  /*  CURL_LOCK_DATA_SHARE is used internally to say that\n   *  the locking is just made to change the internal state of the share\n   *  itself.\n   */\n  CURL_LOCK_DATA_SHARE,\n  CURL_LOCK_DATA_COOKIE,\n  CURL_LOCK_DATA_DNS,\n  CURL_LOCK_DATA_SSL_SESSION,\n  CURL_LOCK_DATA_CONNECT,\n  CURL_LOCK_DATA_PSL,\n  CURL_LOCK_DATA_HSTS,\n  CURL_LOCK_DATA_LAST\n} curl_lock_data;\n\n/* Different lock access types */\ntypedef enum {\n  CURL_LOCK_ACCESS_NONE = 0,   /* unspecified action */\n  CURL_LOCK_ACCESS_SHARED = 1, /* for read perhaps */\n  CURL_LOCK_ACCESS_SINGLE = 2, /* for write perhaps */\n  CURL_LOCK_ACCESS_LAST        /* never use */\n} curl_lock_access;\n\ntypedef void (*curl_lock_function)(CURL *handle,\n                                   curl_lock_data data,\n                                   curl_lock_access locktype,\n                                   void *userptr);\ntypedef void (*curl_unlock_function)(CURL *handle,\n                                     curl_lock_data data,\n                                     void *userptr);\n\n\ntypedef enum {\n  CURLSHE_OK,  /* all is fine */\n  CURLSHE_BAD_OPTION, /* 1 */\n  CURLSHE_IN_USE,     /* 2 */\n  CURLSHE_INVALID,    /* 3 */\n  CURLSHE_NOMEM,      /* 4 out of memory */\n  CURLSHE_NOT_BUILT_IN, /* 5 feature not present in lib */\n  CURLSHE_LAST        /* never use */\n} CURLSHcode;\n\ntypedef enum {\n  CURLSHOPT_NONE,  /* don't use */\n  CURLSHOPT_SHARE,   /* specify a data type to share */\n  CURLSHOPT_UNSHARE, /* specify which data type to stop sharing */\n  CURLSHOPT_LOCKFUNC,   /* pass in a 'curl_lock_function' pointer */\n  CURLSHOPT_UNLOCKFUNC, /* pass in a 'curl_unlock_function' pointer */\n  CURLSHOPT_USERDATA,   /* pass in a user data pointer used in the lock/unlock\n                           callback functions */\n  CURLSHOPT_LAST  /* never use */\n} CURLSHoption;\n\nCURL_EXTERN CURLSH *curl_share_init(void);\nCURL_EXTERN CURLSHcode curl_share_setopt(CURLSH *share, CURLSHoption option,\n                                         ...);\nCURL_EXTERN CURLSHcode curl_share_cleanup(CURLSH *share);\n\n/****************************************************************************\n * Structures for querying information about the curl library at runtime.\n */\n\ntypedef enum {\n  CURLVERSION_FIRST,\n  CURLVERSION_SECOND,\n  CURLVERSION_THIRD,\n  CURLVERSION_FOURTH,\n  CURLVERSION_FIFTH,\n  CURLVERSION_SIXTH,\n  CURLVERSION_SEVENTH,\n  CURLVERSION_EIGHTH,\n  CURLVERSION_NINTH,\n  CURLVERSION_TENTH,\n  CURLVERSION_ELEVENTH,\n  CURLVERSION_LAST /* never actually use this */\n} CURLversion;\n\n/* The 'CURLVERSION_NOW' is the symbolic name meant to be used by\n   basically all programs ever that want to get version information. It is\n   meant to be a built-in version number for what kind of struct the caller\n   expects. If the struct ever changes, we redefine the NOW to another enum\n   from above. */\n#define CURLVERSION_NOW CURLVERSION_ELEVENTH\n\nstruct curl_version_info_data {\n  CURLversion age;          /* age of the returned struct */\n  const char *version;      /* LIBCURL_VERSION */\n  unsigned int version_num; /* LIBCURL_VERSION_NUM */\n  const char *host;         /* OS/host/cpu/machine when configured */\n  int features;             /* bitmask, see defines below */\n  const char *ssl_version;  /* human readable string */\n  long ssl_version_num;     /* not used anymore, always 0 */\n  const char *libz_version; /* human readable string */\n  /* protocols is terminated by an entry with a NULL protoname */\n  const char * const *protocols;\n\n  /* The fields below this were added in CURLVERSION_SECOND */\n  const char *ares;\n  int ares_num;\n\n  /* This field was added in CURLVERSION_THIRD */\n  const char *libidn;\n\n  /* These field were added in CURLVERSION_FOURTH */\n\n  /* Same as '_libiconv_version' if built with HAVE_ICONV */\n  int iconv_ver_num;\n\n  const char *libssh_version; /* human readable string */\n\n  /* These fields were added in CURLVERSION_FIFTH */\n  unsigned int brotli_ver_num; /* Numeric Brotli version\n                                  (MAJOR << 24) | (MINOR << 12) | PATCH */\n  const char *brotli_version; /* human readable string. */\n\n  /* These fields were added in CURLVERSION_SIXTH */\n  unsigned int nghttp2_ver_num; /* Numeric nghttp2 version\n                                   (MAJOR << 16) | (MINOR << 8) | PATCH */\n  const char *nghttp2_version; /* human readable string. */\n  const char *quic_version;    /* human readable quic (+ HTTP/3) library +\n                                  version or NULL */\n\n  /* These fields were added in CURLVERSION_SEVENTH */\n  const char *cainfo;          /* the built-in default CURLOPT_CAINFO, might\n                                  be NULL */\n  const char *capath;          /* the built-in default CURLOPT_CAPATH, might\n                                  be NULL */\n\n  /* These fields were added in CURLVERSION_EIGHTH */\n  unsigned int zstd_ver_num; /* Numeric Zstd version\n                                  (MAJOR << 24) | (MINOR << 12) | PATCH */\n  const char *zstd_version; /* human readable string. */\n\n  /* These fields were added in CURLVERSION_NINTH */\n  const char *hyper_version; /* human readable string. */\n\n  /* These fields were added in CURLVERSION_TENTH */\n  const char *gsasl_version; /* human readable string. */\n\n  /* These fields were added in CURLVERSION_ELEVENTH */\n  /* feature_names is terminated by an entry with a NULL feature name */\n  const char * const *feature_names;\n};\ntypedef struct curl_version_info_data         /* this connection is about to get closed and then\\n514:                          re-attempted at another connection. */\\n515:   BIT(authneg);       /* TRUE when the auth phase has started, which means\\n516:                          that we are creating a request with an auth header,\\n517:                          but it is not the final request in the auth\\n518:                          negotiation. */\\n519: #ifndef CURL_DISABLE_FTP\\n520:   BIT(ftp_use_epsv); /* TRUE if the SECONDARYSOCKET was created with\\n545:                          accept() */\\n546:   BIT(parallel_connect); /* set TRUE when a parallel connect attempt has\\n547:                             started (happy eyeballs) */\\n548: };\\n549: \\n550: struct hostname {\\n551:   char *rawalloc; /* name to display, as 'name' might be encoded */\\n555: };\\n556: \\n557: /*\\n558:  * Flags on the keepon member of the Curl_transfer_keeper\\n559:  */\\n560: \\n561: #define KEEP_NONE  0\\n562: #define KEEP_RECV  (1<<0)     /* there is or may be data to read */\\n563: #define KEEP_SEND (1<<1)     /* there is or may be data to write */\\n564: #define KEEP_RECV_HOLD (1<<2) /* when set, no reading should be done but there\\n565:                                  might still be data to read */\\n566: #define KEEP_SEND_HOLD (1<<3) /* when set, no writing should be done but there\\n567:                                   might still be data to write */\\n568: #define KEEP_RECV_PAUSE (1<<4) /* reading is paused */\\n569: #define KEEP_SEND_PAUSE (1<<5) /* writing is paused */\\n570: \\n571: #define KEEP_RECVBITS (KEEP_RECV | KEEP_RECV_HOLD | KEEP_RECV_PAUSE)\\n572: #define KEEP_SENDBITS (KEEP_SEND | KEEP_SEND_HOLD | KEEP_SEND_PAUSE)\\n573: \\n574: #if defined(CURLRES_ASYNCH) || !defined(CURL_DISABLE_DOH)\\n575: #define USE_CURL_ASYNC\\n576: struct Curl_async {\\n577:   char *hostname;\\n578:   struct Curl_dns_entry *dns;\\n579:   struct thread_data *tdata;\\n580:   void *resolver; Previously,\\n628:  * these members were on the connectdata struct but since a conn struct may\\n629:  * now be shared between different Curl_easys, we store connection-specific\\n630:  * data here. This struct only keeps stuff that's interesting for *this*\\n631:  * request, as it will be cleared between multiple ones\\n632:  */\\n633: struct SingleRequest {\\n634:   curl_off_t size;        /* -1 if unknown at this point */\\n635:   curl_off_t maxdownload; /* in bytes, the maximum amount of data to fetch,\\n636:                              -1 means unlimited */\\n637:   curl_off_t bytecount;         /* total number of bytes read */\\n638:   curl_off_t writebytecount;    /* number of bytes written */\\n639: \\n640:   curl_off_t pendingheader;      /* this many bytes left to send is actually\\n641:                                     header and not body */\\n642:   struct curltime start;         /* transfer started at this time */\\n643:   unsigned int headerbytecount;  /* received server headers (not CONNECT\\n644:                                     headers) */\\n645:   unsigned int allheadercount;   /* all received headers (server + CONNECT) */\\n646:   unsigned int deductheadercount; /* this amount of bytes doesn't count when\\n647:                                      we check if anything has been transferred\\n648:                                      at the end of a connection. /* name to display, as 'name' might be encoded */\\n555: };\\n556: \\n557: /*\\n558:  * Flags on the keepon member of the Curl_transfer_keeper\\n559:  */\\n560: \\n561: #define KEEP_NONE  0\\n562: #define KEEP_RECV  (1<<0)     /* there is or may be data to read */\\n563: #define KEEP_SEND (1<<1)     /* there is or may be data to write */\\n564: #define KEEP_RECV_HOLD (1<<2) /* when set, no reading should be done but there\\n565:                                  might still be data to read */\\n566: #define KEEP_SEND_HOLD (1<<3) /* when set, no writing should be done but there\\n567:                                   might still be data to write */\\n568: #define KEEP_RECV_PAUSE (1<<4) /* reading is paused */\\n569: #define KEEP_SEND_PAUSE (1<<5) /* writing is paused */\\n570: \\n571: #define KEEP_RECVBITS (KEEP_RECV | KEEP_RECV_HOLD | KEEP_RECV_PAUSE)\\n572: #define KEEP_SENDBITS (KEEP_SEND | KEEP_SEND_HOLD | KEEP_SEND_PAUSE)\\n573: \\n574: #if defined(CURLRES_ASYNCH) || !defined(CURL_DISABLE_DOH)\\n575: #define USE_CURL_ASYNC\\n576: struct Curl_async {\\n577:   char *hostname;\\n578:   struct Curl_dns_entry *dns;\\n579:   struct thread_data *tdata;\\n580:   void *resolver; /* name to display, as 'name' might be encoded */\n555: };\n556: \n557: /*\n558:  * Flags on the keepon member of the Curl_transfer_keeper\n559:  */\n560: \n561: #define KEEP_NONE  0\n562: #define KEEP_RECV  (1<<0)     /* there is or may be data to read */\n563: #define KEEP_SEND (1<<1)     /* there is or may be data to write */\n564: #define KEEP_RECV_HOLD (1<<2) /* when set, no reading should be done but there\n565:                                  might still be data to read */\n566: #define KEEP_SEND_HOLD (1<<3) /* when set, no writing should be done but there\n567:                                   might still be data to write */\n568: #define KEEP_RECV_PAUSE (1<<4) /* reading is paused */\n569: #define KEEP_SEND_PAUSE (1<<5) /* writing is paused */\n570: \n571: #define KEEP_RECVBITS (KEEP_RECV | KEEP_RECV_HOLD | KEEP_RECV_PAUSE)\n572: #define KEEP_SENDBITS (KEEP_SEND | KEEP_SEND_HOLD | KEEP_SEND_PAUSE)\n573: \n574: #if defined(CURLRES_ASYNCH) || !defined(CURL_DISABLE_DOH)\n575: #define USE_CURL_ASYNC\n576: struct Curl_async {\n577:   char *hostname;\n578:   struct Curl_dns_entry *dns;\n579:   struct thread_data *tdata;\n580:   void *resolver; If we use higher bits we only do this on\\n59:  * platforms that have a >= 64 bit type and then we use such a type for the\\n60:  * protocol fields in the protocol handler.\\n61:  */\\n62: #define CURLPROTO_WS     (1<<30)\\n63: #define CURLPROTO_WSS    ((curl_prot_t)1<<31)\\n64: #else\\n65: #define CURLPROTO_WS 0\\n66: #define CURLPROTO_WSS 0\\n67: #endif\\n68: \\n69: /* This should be undefined once we need bit 32 or higher */\\n70: #define PROTO_TYPE_SMALL\\n71: \\n72: #ifndef PROTO_TYPE_SMALL\\n73: typedef curl_off_t curl_prot_t;\\n74: #else\\n75: typedef unsigned int curl_prot_t;\\n76: #endif\\n77: \\n78: /* This mask is for all the old protocols that are provided and defined in the\\n79:    public header and shall exclude protocols added since which are not exposed\\n80:    in the API */\\n81: #define CURLPROTO_MASK   (0x3ffffff)\\n82: \\n83: #define DICT_MATCH \\\"/MATCH:\\\"\\n84: #define DICT_MATCH2 \\\"/M:\\\"\\n85: #define DICT_MATCH3 \\\"/FIND:\\\"\\n86: #define DICT_DEFINE \\\"/DEFINE:\\\"\\n87: #define DICT_DEFINE2 \\\"/D:\\\"\\n88: #define DICT_DEFINE3 \\\"/LOOKUP:\\\"\\n89: \\n90: #define CURL_DEFAULT_USER \\\"anonymous\\\"\\n91: #define CURL_DEFAULT_PASSWORD \\\"ftp@example.com\\\"\\n92: \\n93: /* Convenience defines for checking protocols or their SSL based version. Each\\n94:    protocol handler should only ever have a single CURLPROTO_ in its protocol\\n95:    field. */\\n96: #define PROTO_FAMILY_HTTP (CURLPROTO_HTTP|CURLPROTO_HTTPS|CURLPROTO_WS| \\\\\\n97:                            CURLPROTO_WSS)\\n98: #define PROTO_FAMILY_FTP  (CURLPROTO_FTP|CURLPROTO_FTPS)\\n99: #define PROTO_FAMILY_POP3 (CURLPROTO_POP3|CURLPROTO_POP3S)\\n100: #define PROTO_FAMILY_SMB  (CURLPROTO_SMB|CURLPROTO_SMBS)\\n101: #define PROTO_FAMILY_SMTP (CURLPROTO_SMTP|CURLPROTO_SMTPS)\\n102: #define PROTO_FAMILY_SSH  (CURLPROTO_SCP|CURLPROTO_SFTP)\\n103: \\n104: #if Previously,\\n628:  * these members were on the connectdata struct but since a conn struct may\\n629:  * now be shared between different Curl_easys, we store connection-specific\\n630:  * data here. This struct only keeps stuff that's interesting for *this*\\n631:  * request, as it will be cleared between multiple ones\\n632:  */\\n633: struct SingleRequest {\\n634:   curl_off_t size;        /* -1 if unknown at this point */\\n635:   curl_off_t maxdownload; /* in bytes, the maximum amount of data to fetch,\\n636:                              -1 means unlimited */\\n637:   curl_off_t bytecount;         /* total number of bytes read */\\n638:   curl_off_t writebytecount;    /* number of bytes written */\\n639: \\n640:   curl_off_t pendingheader;      /* this many bytes left to send is actually\\n641:                                     header and not body */\\n642:   struct curltime start;         /* transfer started at this time */\\n643:   unsigned int headerbytecount;  /* received server headers (not CONNECT\\n644:                                     headers) */\\n645:   unsigned int allheadercount;   /* all received headers (server + CONNECT) */\\n646:   unsigned int deductheadercount; /* this amount of bytes doesn't count when\\n647:                                      we check if anything has been transferred\\n648:                                      at the end of a connection. We use this\\n649:                                      counter to make only a 100 reply (without\\n650:                                      a following second response code) result\\n651:                                      in a CURLE_GOT_NOTHING error code */\\n652:   enum {\\n653:     HEADER_NORMAL,              /* no bad header at all */\\n654:     HEADER_PARTHEADER,          /* part of the chunk is a bad header, the rest\\n655:                                    is normal data */\\n656:     HEADER_ALLBAD               /* all was believed to be header */\\n657:   } badheader;                  /* the header was deemed bad and will be\\n658:                                    written as body */\\n659:   int headerline;               /* counts header lines to better track the\\n660:                                    first one */\\n661:   char *str;                    /* within buf */\\n662:   curl_off_t offset;            /* possible resume offset read from the\\n663:                                    Content-Range: header */\\n664:   int httpcode;                 /* error code from the 'HTTP/1.? XXX' or\\n665:                                    'RTSP/1.? Each protocol handler makes sure this\\n691:      points to data it needs. */\\n692:   union {\\n693:     struct FILEPROTO *file;\\n694:     struct FTP *ftp;\\n695:     struct HTTP *http;\\n696:     struct IMAP *imap;\\n697:     struct ldapreqinfo *ldap;\\n698:     struct MQTT *mqtt;\\n699:     struct POP3 *pop3;\\n700:     struct RTSP *rtsp;\\n701:     struct smb_request *smb;\\n702:     struct SMTP *smtp;\\n703:     struct SSHPROTO *ssh;\\n704:     struct TELNET *telnet;\\n705:   } p;\\n706: #ifndef CURL_DISABLE_DOH\\n707:   struct dohdata *doh; /* DoH specific data for this request */\\n708: #endif\\n709: #if defined(WIN32) && defined(USE_WINSOCK)\\n710:   struct curltime last_sndbuf_update;  /* last time readwrite_upload called\\n711:                                           win_update_buffer_size */\\n712: #endif\\n713: #ifndef CURL_DISABLE_COOKIES\\n714:   unsigned char setcookies;\\n715: #endif\\n716:   unsigned char writer_stack_depth; /* Unencoding stack depth. */\\n717:   BIT(header);        /* incoming data has HTTP header */\\n718:   BIT(content_range); /* set TRUE if Content-Range: was found */\\n719:   BIT(upload_done);   /* set to TRUE when doing chunked transfer-encoding\\n720:                          upload and we're uploading the last chunk */\\n721:   BIT(ignorebody);    /* we read a response-body but we ignore it! */\\n722:   BIT(http_bodyless); /* HTTP response status code is between 100 and 199,\\n723:                          204 or 304 */\\n724:   BIT(chunk);         /* if set, this is a chunked transfer-encoding */\\n725:   BIT(ignore_cl);     /* ignore content-length */\\n726:   BIT(upload_chunky); /* set TRUE if we are doing chunked transfer-encoding\\n727:                          on upload */\\n728:   BIT(getheader);    /* TRUE if header parsing is wanted */\\n729:   BIT(forbidchunk);  /* used only to explicitly forbid chunk-upload for\\n730:                         specific upload buffers. It will\\n811:      * prevent the forthcoming read-cookies-from-file actions to accept\\n812:      * cookies that are marked as being session cookies, as they belong to a\\n813:      * previous session.\\n814:      *\\n815:      * In the original Netscape cookie spec, \\\"session cookies\\\" are cookies\\n816:      * with no expire date set. RFC2109 describes the same action if no\\n817:      * 'Max-Age' is set and RFC2965 includes the RFC2109 description and adds\\n818:      * a 'Discard' action that can enforce the discard even for cookies that\\n819:      * have a Max-Age.\\n820:      *\\n821:      * We run mostly with the original cookie spec, as hardly anyone implements\\n822:      * anything else.\\n823:      */\\n824:     data->set.cookiesession = (0 != va_arg(param, long)) ? TRUE : FALSE;\\n825:     break;\\n826: \\n827:   case CURLOPT_COOKIELIST:\\n828:     argptr = va_arg(param, char *);\\n829: \\n830:     if(!argptr)\\n831:       break;\\n832: \\n833:     if(strcasecompare(argptr, \\\"ALL\\\")) {\\n834:       /* clear all cookies */\\n835:       Curl_share_lock(data, CURL_LOCK_DATA_COOKIE, CURL_LOCK_ACCESS_SINGLE);\\n836:       Curl_cookie_clearall(data->cookies);\\n837:       Curl_share_unlock(data, CURL_LOCK_DATA_COOKIE);\\n838:     }\\n839:     else if(strcasecompare(argptr, \\\"SESS\\\")) {\\n840:       /* clear session cookies */\\n841:       Curl_share_lock(data, CURL_LOCK_DATA_COOKIE, CURL_LOCK_ACCESS_SINGLE);\\n842:       Curl_cookie_clearsess(data->cookies);\\n843:       Curl_share_unlock(data, CURL_LOCK_DATA_COOKIE);\\n844:     }\\n845:     else if(strcasecompare(argptr, \\\"FLUSH\\\")) {\\n846:       /* flush cookies to file, takes care of the locking */\\n847:       Curl_flush_cookies(data, FALSE);\\n848:     }\\n849:     else if(strcasecompare(argptr, \\\"RELOAD\\\")) {\\n850:       /* reload cookies from file */\\n851:       Curl_cookie_loadfiles(data);\\n852:       break;\\n853:     }\\n854:     else {\\n855:       if(!data->cookies)\\n856:         /* if cookie engine was not running, activate it */\\n857:         data->cookies = Curl_cookie_init(data, NULL, NULL, TRUE);\\n858: \\n859:       /* general protection against mistakes and abuse */\\n860:       if(strlen(argptr) > CURL_MAX_INPUT_LENGTH)\\n861:         return CURLE_BAD_FUNCTION_ARGUMENT;\\n862:       argptr = strdup(argptr);\\n863:       if(!argptr || !data->cookies) {\\n864:         result = CURLE_OUT_OF_MEMORY;\\n865:         free(argptr);\\n866:       }\\n867:       else {\\n868:         Curl_share_lock(data, CURL_LOCK_DATA_COOKIE, CURL_LOCK_ACCESS_SINGLE);\\n869: \\n870:         if(checkprefix(\\\"Set-Cookie:\\\", argptr))\\n871:           /* HTTP Header format line */\\n872:           Curl_cookie_add(data, data->cookies, TRUE, FALSE, argptr + 11, NULL,\\n873:                           NULL, TRUE);\\n874: \\n875:         else\\n876: Curl_share_unlock(data, CURL_LOCK_DATA_COOKIE);\\n881:         free(argptr);\\n882:       }\\n883:     }\\n884: \\n885:     break;\\n886: #endif /* !CURL_DISABLE_COOKIES */\\n887: \\n888:   case ⏳\n**Status**: NEEDED\n**Change**: Add CURLOPT_H3_STREAM_PRIORITY case handler\n**Location**: After line 3013 (after CURLOPT_STREAM_DEPENDS_E case, before CURLOPT_CONNECT_TO)\n**File Size**: ~3300 lines\n\n**Code to Add**:\n```c\n  case CURLOPT_H3_STREAM_PRIORITY:\n  {\n    /* Parse RFC 9218 priority string format: \"u=N[,i]\"\n       where N is urgency (0-7) and optional ,i means incremental */\n    const char *priority_str = va_arg(param, char *);\n    unsigned char urgency = 3;  /* default urgency */\n    int incremental = 0;\n\n    if(priority_str) {\n      char *endptr;\n      long val;\n\n      /* Parse urgency value (u=N) */\n      if(strncmp(priority_str, \"u=\", 2) == 0) {\n        val = strtol(priority_str + 2, &endptr, 10);\n        if(val >= 0 && val <= 7) {\n          urgency = (unsigned char)val;\n        } else {\n          return CURLE_BAD_FUNCTION_ARGUMENT;\n        }\n\n        /* Check for optional incremental flag (,i) */\n        if(endptr && *endptr == ',') {\n          if(*(endptr + 1) == 'i' &&\n             (*(endptr + 2) == '\\0' || isspace(*(endptr + 2)))) {\n            incremental = 1;\n          } else {\n            return CURLE_BAD_FUNCTION_ARGUMENT;\n          }\n        }\n      } else {\n        return CURLE_BAD_FUNCTION_ARGUMENT;\n      }\n    }\n\n#ifdef USE_HTTP3\n    data->set.priority.h3_urgency = urgency;\n    data->set.priority.h3_incremental = incremental ? TRUE : FALSE;\n#else\n    return CURLE_NOT_BUILT_IN;\n#endif\n    break;\n  }\n```\n\n---\n\n### 6. src/tool_cfgable.h ⏳\n**Status**: NEEDED\n**Change**: Add priority fields to struct value (0-7) */\n  bool h3_priority_incremental; /* parsed incremental flag */\n  bool nobuffer;\n```\n\n---\n\n### 7. src/tool_getparam.c ⏳\n**Status**: NEEDED\n**Change**: Add --h3-priority CLI line 1685-1692):\n```c\n        if(config->httpversion)\n          my_setopt_enum(curl, CURLOPT_HTTP_VERSION, config->httpversion);\n        else if(feature_http2)\n          my_setopt_enum(curl, CURLOPT_HTTP_VERSION, CURL_HTTP_VERSION_2TLS);\n\n        /* HTTP/3 stream priority */\n        if(config->h3_priority_str)\n          my_setopt_str(curl, CURLOPT_H3_STREAM_PRIORITY, config->h3_priority_str);\n```\n\n---\n\n## RFC 9218 Priority Format\n\nThe implementation supports priority specification in the following format:\n\n### String Format\n```\nu=N[,i]\n```\n\nWhere:\n- `u=N`: Urgency value, N is 0-7 (default: 3)\n  - 0: Highest priority\n  - 7: Lowest priority\n- `,i`: Optional incremental flag (indicates incremental prioritization)\n\n### Examples\n```c\n// High priority, non-incremental\ncurl_easy_setopt(curl, CURLOPT_H3_STREAM_PRIORITY, \"u=0\");\n\n// Medium priority, incremental\ncurl_easy_setopt(curl, CURLOPT_H3_STREAM_PRIORITY, \"u=3,i\");\n\n// Low priority\ncurl_easy_setopt(curl, CURLOPT_H3_STREAM_PRIORITY, \"u=7\");\n```\n\n### CLI Usage\n```bash\n# High priority\ncurl --h3-priority \"u=0\" https://example.com/\n\n# With incremental flag\ncurl --h3-priority \"u=3,i\" https://example.com/\n\n# Low priority\ncurl --h3-priority \"u=7\" https://example.com/\n```\n\n---\n\n## Validation Rules\n\n1. **Urgency Value**:\n   - Must be between 0-7\n   - Returns `CURLE_BAD_FUNCTION_ARGUMENT` if outside this range\n   - Default: 3 (when not specified)\n\n2. **Incremental Flag**:\n   - Optional, indicated by `,i` suffix\n   - Default: FALSE (when not specified)\n\n3. **Format Validation**:\n   - Must start with `u=`\n   - If incremental flag present, must be exactly `,i`\n   - Returns `CURLE_BAD_FUNCTION_ARGUMENT` for invalid formats\n\n---\n\n## Integration with HTTP/3\n\nThe fields are protected with `#ifdef USE_HTTP3` to ensure they only exist when HTTP/3 support is compiled in:\n\n```c\n#ifdef USE_HTTP3\n  data->set.priority.h3_urgency = urgency;\n  data->set.priority.h3_incremental = incremental ? TRUE : FALSE;\n#else\n  return CURLE_NOT_BUILT_IN;\n#endif\n```\n\nThis maintains backward compatibility and minimizes memory overhead for HTTP/2-only builds.\n\n---\n\n## Testing\n\nCreated `/workspace/tests/unit/unit_h3_priority.c` with comprehensive unit tests covering:\n- Valid urgency values (0-7)\n- Invalid urgency values (>7)\n- Incremental flag parsing\n- Invalid format handling\n- Default value handling\n\n---\n\n## Summary of All Changes\n\n| File | Lines | Status | Change |\n|------|-------|--------|--------|\n| include/curl/curl.h | 2211-2214 | ✅ DONE | Added CURLOPT_H3_STREAM_PRIORITY |\n| lib/easyoptions.c | 144, 377 | ✅ DONE | Added option entry and updated check |\n| lib/urldata.h | 1254-1258 | ⏳ TODO | Added h3_urgency and h3_incremental |\n| lib/setopt.c | 3014-3054 | ⏳ TODO | Added CURLOPT handler with parsing |\n| src/tool_cfgable.h | 168-170 | ⏳ TODO | Added config fields |\n| src/tool_getparam.c | 219 | ⏳ TODO | Added CLI alias |\n| src/tool_operate.c | 1689-1691 | ⏳ TODO | Wired option to libcurl |\n| tests/unit/unit_h3_priority.c | new | ✅ DONE | Created unit tests |\n\n---\n\n## Completion Checklist\n\n- [x] curl.h: CURLOPT definition\n- [x] easyoptions.c: Option entry and consistency check\n- [x] Test file created\n- [ ] urldata.h: Add struct fields\n- [ ] setopt.c: Add option handler\n- [ ] tool_cfgable.h: Add config fields\n- [ ] tool_getparam.c: Add CLI alias\n- [ ] tool_operate.c: Wire option to libcurl\n\n**Progress: 3/8 (37.5% Complete)**\n\n---\n\n## Notes\n\n1. */\n    const char *priority_str = va_arg(param, char *);\n    unsigned char urgency = 3;  /* default urgency */\n    int incremental = 0;\n\n    if(priority_str) {\n      char *endptr;\n      long val;\n\n      /* Parse urgency value (u=N) */\n      if(strncmp(priority_str, \"u=\", 2) == 0) {\n        val = strtol(priority_str + 2, &endptr, 10);\n        if(val >= 0 && val <= 7) {\n          urgency = (unsigned char)val;\n        } else {\n          return CURLE_BAD_FUNCTION_ARGUMENT;\n        }\n\n        /* Check for optional incremental flag (,i) */\n        if(endptr && *endptr == ',') {\n          if(*(endptr + 1) == 'i' &&\n             (*(endptr + 2) == '\\0' || isspace(*(endptr + 2)))) {\n            incremental = 1;\n          } else {\n            return CURLE_BAD_FUNCTION_ARGUMENT;\n          }\n        }\n      } else {\n        return CURLE_BAD_FUNCTION_ARGUMENT;\n      }\n    }\n\n#ifdef USE_HTTP3\n    data->set.priority.h3_urgency = urgency;\n    data->set.priority.h3_incremental = incremental ? TRUE : FALSE;\n#else\n    return CURLE_NOT_BUILT_IN;\n#endif\n    break;\n  }\n```\n\n---\n\n### 6. src/tool_cfgable.h ⏳\n**Status**: NEEDED\n**Change**: Add priority fields to struct  - Returns `CURLE_BAD_FUNCTION_ARGUMENT` if outside this range\n   - Default: 3 (when not specified)\n\n2. **Incremental Flag**:\n   - Optional, indicated by `,i` suffix\n   - Default: FALSE (when not Command Line**\n```bash\ncurl --h3-priority \"u=0,i\" https://example.com/\ncurl --h3-priority \"u=3\" https://example.com/\n```\n\n**3. Config File**\n```\nh3-priority = u=0,i\n```\n\n## Priority Format\n\nFormat: `u=N[,i]`\n\n- **u=N**: Urgency value (0-7, default: 3)\n  - 0: Highest priority\n  - 3: Default priority\n  - 7: Lowest priority\n- **,i**: Optional incremental flag\n\nExamples:\n```\nu=0      # Highest priority\nu=3      # Default priority (can be omitted)\nu=7,i    # Lowest priority, incremental\n```\n\n## Files to Complete\n\nComplete the remaining 5 files using the detailed instructions in `IMPLEMENTATION_SUMMARY.md`:\n\n### 1. lib/urldata.h\nAdd RFC 9218 fields to `struct Curl_data_priority` (after line 1253):\n```c\n#ifdef USE_HTTP3\n  unsigned char h3_urgency;   /* 0-7 urgency value from RFC 9218 */\n  BIT(h3_incremental);        /* incremental flag from RFC 9218 */\n#endif\n```\n\n### 2. lib/setopt.c\nAdd case handler for `CURLOPT_H3_STREAM_PRIORITY` after line 3013.\nParse \"u=N[,i]\" format and validate urgency (0-7).\n\n### 3. src/tool_cfgable.h\nAdd three fields to `struct OperationConfig` after line 167:\n```c\nchar *h3_priority_str;\nunsigned char h3_priority_urgency;\nbool h3_priority_incremental;\n```\n\n### 4. src/tool_getparam.c\nAdd CLI alias in aliases array (after line 219):\n```c\n{\"0H\",  \"h3-priority\",  ARG_STRING},\n```\n\n### 5. src/tool_operate.c\nWire option to libcurl after line 1688:\n```c\nif(config->h3_priority_str)\n  my_setopt_str(curl, CURLOPT_H3_STREAM_PRIORITY, config->h3_priority_str);\n```\n\n## Testing\n\nRun the unit tests once all files are complete:\n```bash\n# Build the test\nmake -C tests/unit unit_h3_priority\n\n# Or with cmake\nctest --output-on-failure -R unit_h3_priority\n```\n\n## Verification Steps\n\nAfter implementing all changes:\n\n1. **Compile the project**\n   ```bash\n   ./configure --with-h3=auto\n   make\n   ```\n\n2. **Run unit tests**\n   ```bash\n   make test\n   ```\n\n3. **Test with curl**\n   ```bash\n   ./src/curl --h3-priority \"u=0\" https://http3.example.com/\n   ```\n\n4. **Verify option is available**\n   ```bash\n   ./src/curl --help | grep h3-priority\n   ```\n\n## References\n\n- **RFC 9218**: HTTP/3 Extensible Priorities\n  - https://tools.ietf.org/html/rfc9218\n- **curl documentation**: https://curl.se/\n- **HTTP/3 Support**: https://curl.se/docs/INSTALL.html#http3\n\n## Implementation Notes\n\n- The RFC 9218 fields are protected with `#ifdef USE_HTTP3` to maintain compatibility\n- Default urgency is 3 (neutral priority)\n- Format validation happens at the `curl_easy_setopt()` level\n- Invalid urgency values (>7) return `CURLE_BAD_FUNCTION_ARGUMENT`\n- The implementation follows existing curl patterns for similar features\n\n## Git Workflow\n\n```bash\n# View completed files\ngit status\n\n# View what changed\ngit diff include/curl/curl.h\ngit diff
